mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with rate declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0%, labor market deterioration signals impending economic contraction, causing investors to discount future corporate earnings and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, or the signal loses predictive power during non-recession periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail auto sales and stable consumer price indices indicate robust consumer demand without inflationary pressure, boosting corporate revenue expectations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 3.0% annually, or interaction term IC remains negative across rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side strength, which translates into immediate positive market sentiment and higher S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns during post-shock recovery phases, or the signal reverses direction when global commodity prices decline sharply.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth exceeding trend levels signals broad economic expansion, increasing corporate profit forecasts and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns show zero or negative correlation with GDPC1 growth rates over any 3-year rolling period, or Rank IC drops below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid monthly inflation spikes trigger expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX yields positive 20-day returns in over 60% of cases when CPIAUCSL MoM exceeds 0.4%, or the signal loses significance during disinflationary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and declining unemployment rates confirm a strengthening labor market, supporting consumer spending and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when wage growth outpaces productivity, or interaction IC remains negative across rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts surpassing 1.2 million units indicate strong residential investment and construction activity, boosting short-term market optimism and positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: HOUST levels above 1200.0 fail to predict positive 5-day returns during housing recovery phases, or the signal reverses when mortgage rates exceed 5.0%.
